mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate rising consumer demand, which boosts corporate revenues and equity valuations. An upward trend in RSAFS produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS level (or 1-month change) vs 20-day forward SPX return remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the correlation flips negative in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate rises above 1.5%, it signals a tightening monetary policy cycle that increases the discount rate on future equity cash flows, compressing valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In triggered instances where FEDFUNDS > 1.5%, the median 60-day forward SPX return is non-negative, or the binary accuracy of the signal is below 55% over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5%) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 2.5%) create a classic late-cycle overheating condition. The joint signal suggests imminent monetary tightening and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4.5% and CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% simultaneously, the median 20-day SPX return is positive, or the interaction term in a linear regression has a coefficient sign inconsistent with the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: During a demand-pull recovery regime, rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects real output growth that validates earlier spending increases. This supply-side confirmation reinforces equity market optimism and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: In periods following a recession trough (GDPC1 trough + 12 months), the correlation between INDPRO 3-month growth and 60-day SPX returns is negative or below 0.15 over any 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, the labor market is at full employment, reducing slack and increasing wage pressure. This raises the probability of preemptive monetary tightening and erodes profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE < 4.0%, the median 20-day forward SPX return is positive, or the hit rate on negative returns is below 50% over any 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive growth in housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals a broad-based expansion in both residential investment and manufacturing. This joint demand-driven momentum tends to lift overall economic sentiment and corporate earnings, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: In periods when both HOUST and INDPRO are above their 6-month moving averages, the median 60-day SPX return is negative or the pairwise correlation of growth rates with returns is below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During a recessionary regime (as proxied by falling or very low PAYEMS), continued declines in nonfarm payrolls indicate deepening economic contraction and deteriorating corporate earnings. This negative labor demand signal drives S&P 500 returns lower over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where PAYEMS declines year-over-year, the median 60-day SPX return is positive, or the time-series rank correlation between PAYEMS year-over-year change and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the CPI index (CPIAUCSL) to above a 2.5% year-over-year threshold signals rising inflation pressures that may reduce real earnings growth and increase the likelihood of imminent rate hikes. Over the short horizon of 5 trading days, equity markets react negatively to such inflation scares.
failure_condition: On days following a CPI release that triggers the condition, the average 5-day SPX return is positive, or the proportion of negative forward 5-day returns is below 55% over a 3-year walk-forward sample.
